entry.title: How Businesses Should Prepare for a Process Audit: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Process audits are pivotal for businesses aiming to ensure efficiency, compliance, and continuous improvement in their operations. Whether conducted internally or by external auditors, these assessments scrutinize organizational processes to identify strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities for enhancement. Here’s a detailed look at how businesses can effectively prepare for a process audit, the types of questions typically asked, common issues encountered, and strategies to ensure a successful audit outcome.

Photo by Scott Graham on Unsplash

Understanding Process Audits

Overall, the intention of a process audit is to provide assurance that organizational processes are well-designed, effectively implemented, and continuously improved to support the organization's strategic objectives and deliver value to stakeholders. It serves as a critical tool for maintaining and enhancing operational excellence, compliance, and overall business performance.

To do this, auditors will evaluate the adherence of operational processes to defined standards, regulations, as well as organizational policies. Audits may also encompass various areas such as quality management, compliance with industry standards, information security protocols, and more.

Steps to Prepare for a Process Audit

To help prepare, consider the following categories:

  1. Documentation Review:
  • Ensure all process documentation (i.e.: procedures, policies, manuals) is up-to-date, accessible, and aligned with current practices.
  • Verify that documentation accurately reflects the sequence of activities, responsibilities, and controls within each process.
  1. Training and Competence:
  • Confirm that personnel involved in audited processes are adequately trained, competent, and possess required certifications or qualifications.
  • Maintain records of training sessions, certifications, and assessments to demonstrate competence.
  1. Process Mapping and Flowcharts:
    • Create or update process maps and flowcharts to visually represent the responsibilities, sequence of steps, decision points, and interactions within each process.
    • Use these visual aids to illustrate process dependencies (i.e. templates, forms, systems, etc.), inputs, outputs, and controls.
  2. Performance Metrics and KPIs:
    • Define clear performance metrics and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for audited processes.
    • Regularly monitor and report on these metrics to track process performance and identify areas needing improvement.
  3. Risk Assessment and Mitigation:
    •  Conduct a thorough risk assessment for audited processes to identify potential vulnerabilities, hazards, or compliance risks.
    • Implement risk mitigation strategies and controls to minimize risks and ensure process resilience.
  4. Internal Audits and Reviews:
    • Perform regular internal audits or reviews of audited processes to proactively identify issues, non-conformities, or opportunities for improvement.
    • Address findings from internal audits promptly and implement corrective actions as necessary.

Types of Questions Typically Asked During a Process Audit

When auditors arrive, they will have many questions about your process. These questions are designed to assess the robustness and effectiveness of your processes, as well as your organization’s ability to adhere to them. Here are some common types of questions you (and your process performers) should be able to answer:

  • Process Understanding:
    • What is the purpose and objective of this process?
    • Can you describe the sequence of activities involved in this process?
    • How do you ensure that the process achieves its intended outcomes?
  • Documentation and Compliance:
    • Where can the documented procedures for this process be found?
    • When was the last time the process documentation was updated?
    •  How do you ensure compliance with relevant standards, regulations, or company policies?
  • Performance and Monitoring:
    • What are the key performance indicators (KPIs) for this process?
    • How frequently are these KPIs monitored and reported?
    • What actions are taken if performance targets are not met?
  • Risk Management:
    • What are the potential risks associated with this process?
    • How are these risks assessed and mitigated?
    • Have there been any incidents or issues related to this process? How were they addressed?
  • Continuous Improvement:
    • How do you identify opportunities for process improvement?
    • Can you provide examples of recent improvements made to this process?
    • How are suggestions or feedback for process improvement evaluated and implemented?

Strategies to Ensure a Successful Audit Outcome

To enhance the likelihood of passing a process audit with flying colors, consider the following strategies:

  • Proactive Preparation: Regularly update documentation, conduct internal audits, and ensure ongoing compliance with standards.
  • Clear Communication: Ensure stakeholders understand their roles, responsibilities, and the importance of audit readiness.
  • Training and Development: Invest in continuous training to maintain a skilled workforce and improve process execution.
  • Robust Monitoring: Implement robust monitoring systems for early detection of issues and proactive corrective actions.
    Embrace Feedback: Use audit findings constructively to drive improvements and strengthen processes over time.

As we have read, when preparing for process audits, it is important to address potential issues proactively, and demonstrate a commitment to continuous improvement. In doing so, businesses can not only pass audits successfully but also enhance operational efficiency and compliance in the long term. 

Process audits should be viewed, not as a necessary evil or an internal witch hunt for issues, but as valuable opportunities to refine operations, mitigate risks, and ultimately, achieve sustainable business success.

— — — — — — — — — — — -

If you found this article helpful, be sure to click like. And of course, follow me for more on the topics of AI, BPM, Process Mining, Customer Experience, Digital Transformation, and Automation.

Until next time — Keep Transforming 💡

entry.title: Re: Dividing different units in advanced formula
entry.content:
@carsten4711 

Using variables to store values from RESULTLOOKUP is not a good practice as these variables are additive and they are not cleared for each record. So if the code runs for 2 or months, all your variables will keep getting added. Using variables to store a constant value is a good use case.

Why not the below directly in line 19

DATA([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202412", [d/PD_Business_Area] = "CSO") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411")

 

or even better to make it scalable. YYYY.PP average will be calculated using previous months salary and FTE

MEMBERSET [d/Account] = ("Opex - Salaries & Wages", "FHeads_FJEmps")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202411" TO "202412"
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Business_Area] = ("CSO")
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Cost_Center] = ("1000/#")

DATA([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))

 

In above code 202411 will be calculated using 202410 and 202412 will be calculated using 202411

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Manage characterstics as custom fields in Fiori apps
entry.content:
Overview:

The business use case/ requirement which usually is requested by almost all organizations using variant configuration as a core solution in their processes is to have these VC characteristics very easily available across many reporting/monitoring tools specifically while monitoring production orders. As a result, we tend to add an enhancement in transactions COOIS/COHV to aid this. As Fiori’s adoption with S4 increases and with SAP recommending clean core to clients globally, we as consultants will need to fall back on low-code/no-code functionalities to manage such customer specific requirements. To address this requirement, we can use two Fiori apps in tandem to add these characteristics in Fiori app “manage production orders”. Manage production orders Fiori app can be used to monitor/manage the overall shop floor activities.

 

Pre-requisites:

  • Configurable Material master & other manufacturing related master data are already setup
  • Fiori apps “Manage Characteristics as custom fields(F4337)”, “Custom fields(F1481)”, “Manage production orders(F2336)” are already activated & required authorizations available
  • Configuration profile for KMAT has been setup with “Advanced variant configuration”
  • Sales order & production is created for the configurable material

Steps to create custom field & adding the field to Fiori app:

Step 1: Create characteristics as a custom field:

  • Open app Manage Characteristics as custom fields, for adding custom fields to Manage production order app, we need to use business context “PRODUCTION_ORDER_CFE”

 

  • Toggle to details & click on edit & add a characteristic, below example, I have added characteristics – COLOUR & save. After saving, wait for some time to allow the system to publish, the field status needs to change to “ready to use”

 

 

 

 

Step 2 – Extend data source to add the custom field:

  • Open Fiori app “Custom fields & Logic”, go to tab “data source extensions” & click on the plus button to add an extension

 

  • Add the field “Colour” by selection

 

  • Save & publish

 

Step 3: Display the added field in app “Manage production orders”

  • Open app manage production orders, now the column for the added charactrstics is available

 

 

Conclusion:

The Fiori app “manage characteristics as custom fields” is a powerful tool for the functional consultants to add in the fields in the Fiori apps. This app also provides a functionality to add batch characteristics to the app for managing batches, which is also will find many use cases in the batch managed production industries. For release information, restriction & issues regarding this app please refer to KBA 3220904.

I hope this blog will help in answering some business requirements. I would appreciate any suggestions or improvements to make this blog more productive.

Thanks, Ninad

entry.title: Re: Different capitalisation dates and years for book and...
entry.content: I think you could settle to all areas, but for area 01 use initially depreciation key 0000 (no depreciation) then once you want to start depreciation on area 01 use a time interval to add the key you want to automate depreciation with.

entry.title: SAP Purchase Material inventory (F) to Manufacture Materials (E)
entry.content: Steps to rework SAP Purchase Material inventory (F) to Manufacture Materials (E)

entry.title: Sybase ASE 16.0.03.11 truncate table partition very slow when archiving data using NLS
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are using NLS to archive data from SAP BW NetWeaver 7.4 SP 13 on Sybase ASE 16.0.03.11 to Sybase IQ.

When trying to archive an info provider which is a semantically partitioned object based on DSOs, we are running into an issue during the deletion phase of the archiving process. The copy and verification process are good, but during the deletion phase, the following statement takes around 7 hours to run per partition and the job takes days to finish. The strange thing is some of the partitions are empty and have no data as well and still it takes around 7 hours to finish.

TRUNCATE TABLE "xxxx" PARTITION "xxxx"

Can someone provide step by step instructions on how to go about investigating this issue. I would like to understand how to record traces on Sybase ASE 16 16.0.03.11 to understand what's causing this slowness on the database.

Thank you and appreciate any help on this !

entry.title: Re: Manual Distribution Rule
entry.content: Hi Ankit, sorry to bother, but has anything changed since this was posted? I'm currently facing the same challenge and not being able to fill a Manual Distribution rule via DI API is gonna be troublesome. Do you know of any workaround to make this work? Thanks for your support

entry.title: Solman Introscope Enterprise Manager own certificate
entry.content:
Dear Support
I have configured Wily Introscope Enterprise Manager to use https.
It works fine. My question is, is it possible to change the default keystore to a self signed keystore?



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solman Introscope Enterprise Manager own certificate
entry.content:
It is possible to use your Own Certificate and generate a server key/certificate for HTTPS usage.

Please follow the information available at support.sap.com : Enable HTTPS for Introscope Enterprise Manager (sap.com)

entry.title: Could not get the ApiHub resource data

I have logged in API Hub quite a few times to ensure that my credentials are right but so far I simply don't seem to get access through BAS.

Any pointers please....?

What do I need to do get this working?

entry.title: Re: The automatic creation of the asset master data durin...
entry.content: Thank you Mark. I am still looking for a solution. Having the first step in the asset purchase process be the creation of the asset doesn't make sense to us. Then we need a workflow for users to request the creation of an asset in the approved area.

entry.title: ODM: Issue trying to Activate/Generate OEIT order component
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I have an issue with ODM tables. Add new custom fields (/1OM/ORDROEIT*) component OEIT of the structure /SCMB/ODM_ORI_EXT_STR.

It displayed an error "The structure of component OEIT could not be generated (DDIF_TABL_ACTIVATE) when I try to activate and on generate a warning about "unnecessary key fields" 

Actions:  Added the new fields using an append structure and activate/generate the order component, data type and data area.

Analysis:

  • I noticed that the field of the component OEIT “Date/Time Activ.”  never changed and the version logs was changed.
  • The not extra key fields exist in the structure /SCMB/ODM_ORI_EXT_STR
  • The version of the tables and structures were updated. 

New Plan:

  • Removed all the new custom fields.
  • Procced with the Activation/ Generation.
  • Same results and same issues.

Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: The upload file name is missing in object page when f...
entry.content: All good, I tried it and it worked!

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 2 - Words as Ve
Re: Showing Partial Dimension Members in SAC Chart Using...
entry.content:
Hi,

Try creating this partial dimension members from backend (SAP BW system) and bring it in a separate dimension to display in your widget in SAC.

Thanks!

Venkat

entry.title: How to mass convert Quotations into Sales Orders
entry.content:
Daily, we have a huge amount of Quotations to be converted into Sales Orders. For this matter, we need a mass processing tool to convert them, because doing it one by one it's too time consuming.

How can we convert Sales Quotations into Sales Orders massively?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass convert Quotations into Sales Orders
entry.content:
There are 2 ways to mass create Sales Orders with reference to a Sales Quotation:

 

1. Using the Sales Order (A2A) API.

You can either create an independent sales order or create a sales order with reference to a preceding document.

For more details, check the documentation: Create Sales Orders with Reference | SAP Help Portal

 

2. Using the Import Sales Orders app

You can use this app to create sales orders from spreadsheets. You can import document data to create sales documents with reference to a preceding document.

For more details, check the documentation: Import Sales Orders | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: I need SAP BTP SDK version 4.0.5. Can you please provide the link?
entry.content:
Hi anujsoni,

Any components available to you can be downloaded from the "Download Software" section of SAP for Me (https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter). Direct download links outside of SAP for Me cannot be provided. 

Regards,

Mark Fogle
SAP BTP SDK for Android

entry.title: Solution Manager 7.2 - Editing templates for System Monitoring
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

when I try to Edit monitoring templates for System Monitoring in transaction solman_setup as user solman_admin, I am getting the following error:

Which role should I assign in order to be able to edit monitoring templates ? With SAP_ALL and SAP_NEW profiles it works, of course, but this is not a clean solution. Please advise! Thank you!

entry.title: PaPM On Premise - Dual Control with Simulation Process Template
entry.content:
Hi All,

Is it possible to manage the order of activities if the process template is set as "Simulation"? (Example: If task A001 was not completed, prevent task A002 from running.) Because I noticed that this is possible if the process template is set as "Run" by setting the "previous activity" column but this type of template does not allow the possibility of modifying the parameters.

Thank you,
Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Integration of My Inbox Fiori application with SAP Launchpad service

Hi @SomaskandanK ,

We have a similar requirement, we want to integrate BTP-Build process automation (BPA) my Inbox app with Fiori My inbox for our S4 HANA public cloud system, so that the Work items generated for BTP-BPA myinbox app can shown on Fiori MyInbox as well for users and they can act on those work items.

Could you please suggest us whether its possible and if yes what  will be the approach to follow for the same.

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: Use of abap class " CL_ABAP_CONV_IN_CE" is not permited. I am using S/$HANA public cloud.Can you please give an alternative solution?

entry.title: if you want to go a bit further than the SAP trainings on AI and understand how SAP JOULE works
entry.content:
If you are interested in all the innovations related to AI, you might also have attended the new trainings around SAP GenAI HUB  and SAP JOULE
And maybe you have also been asking yourself "Okay, but how does it work under the hood, what is the magic behind all this ?"

There are tons of content available freely on this topic, but the quality can be ..... limited (generated by ChatGPT ?)

So in case you are interested, here are the two best content I found for a technical explanations on the main concepts that SAP JOULE is based on:

- for a detailled introduction on LLMs (one hour but worth it  …): Intro to Large Language Models - YouTube

- to understand how SAP JOULE will add the context of the company data from their respective SAP systems and Microsoft applications, via a processus called Retrieval Augmented Generation ( or "grounding"), a short and very clear presentation by don woodlock: What is RAG? (Retrieval Augmented Generation) (youtube.com)

enjoy the show and please add your own finds !  🍿

(note: original posted on Linkedin)

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Extended ECM (xECM) by OpenText- An overview
entry.content:
Thank you @Ajinkya1 for this blog.

Quick question is it possible to specified the documentsthat I would like to go to open text? For example I just would like to send to open text candidates files in the RCM module.

 

Thank you

entry.title: How to set a delivery block on schedule line
entry.content:
We need to insert a delivery block on the schedule line.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set a delivery block on schedule line
entry.content:
1. Go to the app 'Change Sales Orders' (VA02)
  2. Select the desired document
  3. Select the item you want to block and click on 'Display Item Details'
  4. Go to the tab 'Schedule Lines'
  5. Select the Delivery Block on the table field
  6. Click save

entry.content: Up

entry.content: up

entry.title: Divergência de volume - na emissão de solicitações de notas STO 861
entry.content:
Boa tarde pessoal!

Quando vou criar uma delivery, faço o Pedido de compra, depois o VL10B para gerar a delivery e em seguida o VL02N para fechar o processo e emitir a Nota Fiscal em J1BNFE.

Ao gerar a Nota Fiscal, o volume sempre vem a quantidade 1. 

Fiz 5 testes colocando o valor de quantidade do volume EX: 3, 5, 6 e etc.. e sempre na nota emitida a quantidade vem 1.

Alguem pode me ajudar de como consigo resolver este caso?

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Cloud ALM with SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content: Thanks Jagmohan for the information about CBC.

entry.title: Re: Divergência de volume - na emissão de solicitações de notas STO 861

entry.title: Re: Goods Return without GRPO
entry.content:
Dear @ali93 ,

If you are not able to distinctly identify the stock quantity that you ought to return, you should check with vendor, if the item is managed by serial no., otherwise you may choose any of the Goods Receipt from the Goods Return document. Then inform the vendor about the return with appropriate reasons and GRPO nos that you have used for Return (as reference), thereby your vendor could also process them accordingly.

Thanks.

entry.title: Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM/YYYY available in SAP?
entry.content:
Hi,

Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM/YYYY available in SAP?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Follow the steps below:

  1. On the upper right side, there's a profile button, the one that shows the user initial words. You need to click on this button
  2. Click on "Settings" 
  3. Click on "Language and Region"
  4. Check the "Date Format" field 
  5. You can choose "DD.MM.YYYY"

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 4 - Geospatial
entry.content: Weight (number of hops): 1

entry.title: Re: How to enable design mode on a desktop to adjust RF s...
entry.content: @ACHEKKA I think the best course of action would be creating a support incident directed to the EWM-RF team to help out with the design mode, since this is not a feature related to SAP Screen Personas.

entry.title: Is it possible to report CBTA test results at configuration test case level on Composite scripts?
entry.content:
Is it possible to report CBTA test results at the configuration test case/script level with the individual configuration scripts being included in the Composite scripts?  I'm executing a Test Plan/Test Package automatically/immediately.

For example, say I have Composite script "ZCOMP_SAP_OTC_Create, change and display sales order" and the Test Script Steps include 1. VA01 - Create Sales Order, 2. VA02 Change Sales Order and 3. VA03 Display Sales Order.  When I execute all my scripts within a Test Plan/Test Package currently the test status reporting is from the composite level only.  So, if only the VA03 fails, then the test result that is report is an overall failure for the composite script.  In CBTA, is there a way to display results (pass/fail) for each of the test script steps with the composite script (in this example VA01, VA02 and VA03) instead of only reporting from the composite script level?

I've checked configurable reports, logs, Test Package Status tab, but I don't see a way...  Below is the Status tab of a Test Package that displays the composition scripts failed (no individual test case status), and this is how all the reporting seems to behave.

entry.title: loadProcedure is not a function
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working with an MTA (multi-target application) within an on-premise HANA XSA with WebIDE. 

In my project I have a db module with a .hdbprocedure artifact and I'm trying to run it from a xsjs module.

In the xsjs module, I created a country.xsjs where I try to run the procedure. The code is like below:

 

var conn = $.hdb.getConnection();
var fnCreateCountry = conn.loadProcedure("TINYWORLD_HDI_TINYDB_1","tinyworld.tinydb::createCountry");
var result = fnCreateCountry();
conn.commit();
conn.close();

 

The problem when I run the country.xsjs I get the error below:

It seems that it is not finding the loadProcedure under $.hdb.getConnection...

I`m using HANA 2.0 SPS 07.

I did the exactly tinyworld exercise from the HANA developer guide:
SAP HANA Developer Guide for XS Advanced Model (SAP Web IDE) | SAP Help Portal

Appreciate any help on this.

Best Regards,

Thiago

#WebIDE, #HANA XS Advanced, #XSJS

entry.title: Re: ABAP Restful Application Programming : Enabling draft feature to the custom implemented Actions
entry.content:
Hi @Ramjee_korada 

Is there a way to open the business object in Edit mode after clicking a custom action (e.g., Reset button)? The reset button currently has 2 purposes, first is to clear the screen and the other is to open the object in Edit Mode (i.e., Draft State).

I know that we can simply click the Edit button and subsequently click Reset button or vice versa, we are just wondering if it is feasible to do it in a single button click.

I tried applying the logic above but I am getting this error message:

I suspect it has something to do with the URL parameters:

Before clicking Reset button, the UNAME field is filled up with the username value which is the key for this business object.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='RBALAGTA',IsActiveEntity=true)

However, after clicking the Reset button (and refreshing the screen via side effects), the uname field became blank.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='',IsActiveEntity=false)

Here is my code for the Reset action implementation:

 

   READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY Upload
           ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_file)
        ENTITY Upload BY \_Output
          ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_output).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
       ENTITY Upload
       EXECUTE edit FROM
       VALUE #( FOR <fs_active_key> IN keys WHERE ( %is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off )
                                                  ( %key = <fs_active_key>-%key
                                                    %cid = 'RBALAGTA'
                                                    %param-preserve_changes = 'X' ) )
       REPORTED DATA(edit_reported)
       FAILED DATA(edit_failed)
       MAPPED DATA(edit_mapped).

    DATA(lt_keys) = keys.
    LOOP AT lt_keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_key>).
      <ls_key>-%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
    ENDLOOP.

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT lt_file.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
      UPDATE
        FIELDS ( Status
                 Input1
                 Input2
                 Input3
                 Input4
                 Input5
                 Attachment
                 Filename
                 Mimetype
                 Hideexcel
                 Updatedata
                 )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR <fs_rec_draft> IN lt_file ( %tky = <fs_rec_draft>-%tky
                                                    %is_draft = '01'
                                                     Status           = 'R'
                                                                             Input1           = ''
                                                                             Input2           = ''
                                                                             Input3           = ''
                                                                             Input4           = ''
                                                                             Input5           = ''
                                                                             Attachment       = ''
                                                                             Filename         = ''
                                                                             Mimetype         = ''
                                                                             Hideexcel        = ''
                                                                             Updatedata       = abap_false
                                                                             %control-Status  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input1  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input2  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input3  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input4  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input5  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Attachment = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Filename  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Mimetype  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Hideexcel  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-UpdateData = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) )
      ENTITY Output
      DELETE FROM VALUE #( FOR ls_output IN lt_output ( %key = ls_output-%key ) )
                                     REPORTED edit_reported
                                     FAILED edit_failed
                                     MAPPED DATA(lt_updated).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT DATA(lt_new_file).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_file IN lt_new_file
                          ( %tky   = ls_file-%tky
                            %param = ls_file ) ).

 

 

I have added also the behavior definition below.

 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_fileupload unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_fileupload alias Upload
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zdfileupload
etag master uname
lock master
total etag uname
authorization master ( global )
{

  field ( readonly ) Uname, Input6;
  field ( mandatory ) Input1;
  internal create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _output { internal create; with draft; }
  association _Parameters { create; with draft; }

  action processRecords result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
  action (lock : none) resetScreen result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
//  action (lock : none) resetScreen;

  validation validateInput on save { create; update; }
  validation validateInput1 on save { create; field Input1; }
  validation validateAttachment on save { create; field Attachment; }

  determine action valdetInput1 { validation validateInput1; }
  determine action valdetAttachment { validation validateAttachment; }

  side effects {
    action resetScreen affects $self, entity _output;
    action processRecords affects entity _output;
    determine action valdetInput1 executed on field Input1 affects messages;
    determine action valdetAttachment executed on field Attachment affects messages;
  }

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation validateInput;
    validation ( always ) validateInput1;
    validation ( always ) validateAttachment;
  }

}

define behavior for zi_fileoutput alias Output
with additional save
persistent table zfilecontent
draft table zdfilecontent
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  action processData result [0..*] $self;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }

  side effects {
    action processData affects $self;
  }
}

define behavior for zi_uploadparams alias Parameters
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zduploadparams
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }
}

entry.title: Re: Issue with 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE' - Mass Upload
entry.content:
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL'
      EXPORTING
        purchaseorder    = ws_po
        items            = 'X'
        services         = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        po_header        = wa_po_header
      TABLES
        po_items         = po_items
        po_item_services = po_services
        return           = bapi_return_po.
*    BREAK-POINT.
    "------------------------------------------------------------
    IF line_exists( po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ] )..
      pckg_no = po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ]-pckg_no.
      IF line_exists( po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ] )..
        sub_pckg_no =  po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ]-subpckg_no.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    "------------------------------------------------------------
*
    wa_header-po_number = wa_file2-ebeln.
    wa_header-po_item = wa_file2-EBELP.
    wa_header-short_text = wa_file2-short_text.
    wa_header-doc_date = wa_file2-doc_date.
    wa_header-post_date = wa_file2-post_date.
    wa_header-begdate = wa_file2-begdate.
    wa_header-enddate = wa_file2-enddate.
    wa_header-acceptance = wa_file2-acceptance.
    wa_header-pckg_no = 1.
    wa_header-pckg_no = pckg_no.

    serial_no = 0.
    line_no = 1.
*
    bapi_esll-pckg_no = 1.
    bapi_esll-pckg_no = pckg_no.
    bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
    bapi_esll-outl_level = '0'.
    bapi_esll-outl_ind = 'X'.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = 2.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
    APPEND bapi_esll.

    "------------------------------------------------------------
    DATA: ext_line LIKE wa_file-line_no.
    LOOP AT it_file INTO wa_file WHERE ebeln = wa_file2-ebeln AND  ebelp = wa_file2-ebelp.

      IF line_exists( po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no ] )..
        wa_po_services =  po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no  pckg_no = sub_pckg_no ] .
      ENDIF.
      line_no = line_no + 1.
      ext_line = ext_line + 10.
      CLEAR bapi_esll.
 MOVE-CORRESPONDING wa_po_services TO bapi_esll.
* bapi_esll-gr_price = '456'.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = 2.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
      bapi_esll-ext_line =  ext_line  .
      bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
*      bapi_esll-PLN_LINE = line_no.
      bapi_esll-service = wa_po_services-service.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_file-quantity.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_po_services-quantity.
      bapi_esll-subpckg_no = wa_po_services-subpckg_no.
*      bapi_esll-quantity = 1.
      CLEAR: bapi_esll-gr_price ." = wa_po_services-gr_price.
      CLEAR: bapi_esll-price_unit ."= wa_po_services-price_unit.

      APPEND bapi_esll.
    ENDLOOP.


    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE'
      EXPORTING
        entrysheetheader   = wa_header
*       testrun            = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        entrysheet         = ws_entrysheet_no
      TABLES
*       ENTRYSHEETACCOUNTASSIGNMENT =
        entrysheetservices = bapi_esll
*       entrysheetsrvaccassvalues = i_service_acc
        return             = i_return
*       entrysheetservicestexts = i_service_text
*       ENTRYSHEETHEADERTEXT =
      .

entry.title: How to open a business object in Edit mode using a custom action/button via RAP/Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a way to open the business object in Edit mode after clicking a custom action (e.g., Reset button)? The reset button currently has 2 purposes, first is to clear the screen and the other is to open the object in Edit Mode (i.e., Draft State).

I know that we can simply click the Edit button and subsequently click Reset button or vice versa, we are just wondering if it is feasible to do it in a single button click.

I tried applying the logic based on Ramjee's tutorial (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/abap-restful-application-programming-enabling-draft-feature-to-the-custom/bc-p/13750648) but I am getting this error message (although in the background, the draft instance has been created):

I suspect it has something to do with the URL parameters:

Before clicking Reset button, the UNAME field is filled up with the username value which is the key for this business object.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='RBALAGTA',IsActiveEntity=true)

However, after clicking the Reset button (and refreshing the screen via side effects), the uname field became blank.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='',IsActiveEntity=false)

Here is my code for the Reset action implementation:

 

   READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY Upload
           ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_file)
        ENTITY Upload BY \_Output
          ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_output).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
       ENTITY Upload
       EXECUTE edit FROM
       VALUE #( FOR <fs_active_key> IN keys WHERE ( %is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off )
                                                  ( %key = <fs_active_key>-%key
                                                    %cid = 'RBALAGTA'
                                                    %param-preserve_changes = 'X' ) )
       REPORTED DATA(edit_reported)
       FAILED DATA(edit_failed)
       MAPPED DATA(edit_mapped).

    DATA(lt_keys) = keys.
    LOOP AT lt_keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_key>).
      <ls_key>-%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
    ENDLOOP.

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT lt_file.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
      UPDATE
        FIELDS ( Status
                 Input1
                 Input2
                 Input3
                 Input4
                 Input5
                 Attachment
                 Filename
                 Mimetype
                 Hideexcel
                 Updatedata
                 )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR <fs_rec_draft> IN lt_file ( %tky = <fs_rec_draft>-%tky
                                                    %is_draft = '01'
                                                     Status           = 'R'
                                                                             Input1           = ''
                                                                             Input2           = ''
                                                                             Input3           = ''
                                                                             Input4           = ''
                                                                             Input5           = ''
                                                                             Attachment       = ''
                                                                             Filename         = ''
                                                                             Mimetype         = ''
                                                                             Hideexcel        = ''
                                                                             Updatedata       = abap_false
                                                                             %control-Status  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input1  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input2  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input3  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input4  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input5  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Attachment = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Filename  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Mimetype  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Hideexcel  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-UpdateData = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) )
      ENTITY Output
      DELETE FROM VALUE #( FOR ls_output IN lt_output ( %key = ls_output-%key ) )
                                     REPORTED edit_reported
                                     FAILED edit_failed
                                     MAPPED DATA(lt_updated).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT DATA(lt_new_file).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_file IN lt_new_file
                          ( %tky   = ls_file-%tky
                            %param = ls_file ) ).

 

 

 

I have added also the behavior definition below.a

 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_fileupload unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_fileupload alias Upload
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zdfileupload
etag master uname
lock master
total etag uname
authorization master ( global )
{

  field ( readonly ) Uname, Input6;
  field ( mandatory ) Input1;
  internal create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _output { internal create; with draft; }
  association _Parameters { create; with draft; }

  action processRecords result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
  action (lock : none) resetScreen result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
//  action (lock : none) resetScreen;

  validation validateInput on save { create; update; }
  validation validateInput1 on save { create; field Input1; }
  validation validateAttachment on save { create; field Attachment; }

  determine action valdetInput1 { validation validateInput1; }
  determine action valdetAttachment { validation validateAttachment; }

  side effects {
    action resetScreen affects $self, entity _output;
    action processRecords affects entity _output;
    determine action valdetInput1 executed on field Input1 affects messages;
    determine action valdetAttachment executed on field Attachment affects messages;
  }

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation validateInput;
    validation ( always ) validateInput1;
    validation ( always ) validateAttachment;
  }

}

define behavior for zi_fileoutput alias Output
with additional save
persistent table zfilecontent
draft table zdfilecontent
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  action processData result [0..*] $self;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }

  side effects {
    action processData affects $self;
  }
}

define behavior for zi_uploadparams alias Parameters
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zduploadparams
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }
}

entry.title: Re: Issue with 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE' - Mass Upload
entry.content:
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL'
      EXPORTING
        purchaseorder    = ws_po
        items            = 'X'
        services         = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        po_header        = wa_po_header
      TABLES
        po_items         = po_items
        po_item_services = po_services
        return           = bapi_return_po.    IF line_exists( po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ] )..
      pckg_no = po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ]-pckg_no.
      IF line_exists( po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ] )..
        sub_pckg_no =  po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ]-subpckg_no.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
*
    wa_header-po_number = wa_file2-ebeln.
    wa_header-po_item = wa_file2-EBELP.
    wa_header-short_text = wa_file2-short_text.
    wa_header-doc_date = wa_file2-doc_date.
    wa_header-post_date = wa_file2-post_date.
    wa_header-begdate = wa_file2-begdate.
    wa_header-enddate = wa_file2-enddate.
    wa_header-acceptance = wa_file2-acceptance.
    wa_header-pckg_no = 1.
    wa_header-pckg_no = pckg_no.

    serial_no = 0.
    line_no = 1.

    bapi_esll-pckg_no = 1.
    bapi_esll-pckg_no = pckg_no.
    bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
    bapi_esll-outl_level = '0'.
    bapi_esll-outl_ind = 'X'.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = 2.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
    APPEND bapi_esll.

    DATA: ext_line LIKE wa_file-line_no.
    LOOP AT it_file INTO wa_file WHERE ebeln = wa_file2-ebeln AND  ebelp = wa_file2-ebelp.

      IF line_exists( po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no ] )..
        wa_po_services =  po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no  pckg_no = sub_pckg_no ] .
      ENDIF.
      line_no = line_no + 1.
      ext_line = ext_line + 10.
      CLEAR bapi_esll.
 MOVE-CORRESPONDING wa_po_services TO bapi_esll.
* bapi_esll-gr_price = '456'.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = 2.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
      bapi_esll-ext_line =  ext_line  .
      bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
*      bapi_esll-PLN_LINE = line_no.
      bapi_esll-service = wa_po_services-service.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_file-quantity.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_po_services-quantity.
      bapi_esll-subpckg_no = wa_po_services-subpckg_no.
      bapi_esll-gr_price  = wa_po_services-gr_price.
     bapi_esll-price_unit  = wa_po_services-price_unit.

      APPEND bapi_esll.
    ENDLOOP.


    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE'
      EXPORTING
        entrysheetheader   = wa_header
      IMPORTING
        entrysheet         = ws_entrysheet_no
      TABLES
        entrysheetservices = bapi_esll
        return             = i_return
      .

entry.title: Re: How to open a business object in Edit mode using a custom action/button via RAP/Fiori Elements?
entry.content: @Ramjee_korada, maybe you can help. I tried to post this question as a comment to your original post but it was marked as spam, probably after I edit it multiple times.

entry.title: Re: 2024-07 Developer Challenge - Task 0 - Server and service provisioning
entry.content: My service is up and running on Cloud Foundry!

entry.title: Re: 2024-07 Developer Challenge - Task 0 - Server and service provisioning
entry.content: URL: qmacro-simplest-deployment.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

entry.title: Re: Issue with 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE' - Mass Upload
entry.content:
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL'
      EXPORTING
        purchaseorder    = ws_po
        items            = 'X'
        services         = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        po_header        = wa_po_header
      TABLES
        po_items         = po_items
        po_item_services = po_services
        return           = bapi_return_po.    IF line_exists( po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ] )..
      pckg_no = po_items[ po_item = wa_file2-ebelp ]-pckg_no.
      IF line_exists( po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ] )..
        sub_pckg_no =  po_services[ pckg_no = pckg_no ]-subpckg_no.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
*
    wa_header-po_number = wa_file2-ebeln.
    wa_header-po_item = wa_file2-EBELP.
    wa_header-short_text = wa_file2-short_text.
    wa_header-doc_date = wa_file2-doc_date.
    wa_header-post_date = wa_file2-post_date.
    wa_header-begdate = wa_file2-begdate.
    wa_header-enddate = wa_file2-enddate.
    wa_header-acceptance = wa_file2-acceptance.
    wa_header-pckg_no = 1.
    wa_header-pckg_no = pckg_no.

    serial_no = 0.
    line_no = 1.

    bapi_esll-pckg_no = 1.
    bapi_esll-pckg_no = pckg_no.
    bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
    bapi_esll-outl_level = '0'.
    bapi_esll-outl_ind = 'X'.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = 2.
    bapi_esll-subpckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
    APPEND bapi_esll.

    DATA: ext_line LIKE wa_file-line_no.
    LOOP AT it_file INTO wa_file WHERE ebeln = wa_file2-ebeln AND  ebelp = wa_file2-ebelp.

      IF line_exists( po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no ] )..
        wa_po_services =  po_services[ ext_line = wa_file-line_no  pckg_no = sub_pckg_no ] .
      ENDIF.
      line_no = line_no + 1.
      ext_line = ext_line + 10.
      CLEAR bapi_esll.
 MOVE-CORRESPONDING wa_po_services TO bapi_esll.
* bapi_esll-gr_price = '456'.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = 2.
      bapi_esll-pckg_no = sub_pckg_no.
      bapi_esll-ext_line =  ext_line  .
      bapi_esll-line_no = line_no.
*      bapi_esll-PLN_LINE = line_no.
      bapi_esll-service = wa_po_services-service.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_file-quantity.
      bapi_esll-quantity = wa_po_services-quantity.
      bapi_esll-subpckg_no = wa_po_services-subpckg_no.
      bapi_esll-gr_price  = wa_po_services-gr_price.
     bapi_esll-price_unit  = wa_po_services-price_unit.

      APPEND bapi_esll.
    ENDLOOP.


    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ENTRYSHEET_CREATE'
      EXPORTING
        entrysheetheader   = wa_header
      IMPORTING
        entrysheet         = ws_entrysheet_no
      TABLES
        entrysheetservices = bapi_esll
        return             = i_return
      .

entry.title: Re: loadProcedure is not a function
entry.content: don't waste time on xsjs.

entry.title: Re: display full names in order of first name and last name
entry.content: Could you please provide more details about the posting? Which order do you mean? Thank you!

entry.title: how to create a default password for all SF users
entry.content:
Dear All

We are implementing IAS in a new client, but most of their employees are operational, that is, they do not have a corporate email, so we have chosen to create dummy emails, but how can we create a default password for all SF users? ? so they can log in

I have followed the KB https://me.sap.com/notes/0003001615  but it does not work

Can someone help me know what steps are correct to carry out this test?

thank you so much

Greetings

entry.title: Re: Customer return - Data fro creating a delivery is incomplete (Customer) - M7278
entry.content: Added S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain tag, Please remove tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: Validate if the swift/bic field is empty through a BADI in tx fi02
entry.content:
Hi!

Regarding EXIT_SAPLBANK_001, do you mean that I can modify it and create a program there to validate the SWIFT field?  

SMOD SAPLBANK

EXIT_SAPLBANK_001

entry.title: Re: RAP (Fiori Elements) how to make a Criticality for Action Button ?
entry.content: Hi @patrick_winkler i tried it in the XML and from CDS Metadata and did not work

entry.title: Re: RAP (Fiori Elements) how to make a Criticality for Action Button ?
entry.content: @patrick_winkler i am using ODATA V4

entry.title: SAP GRC 12.0 Access Control and Microsoft Entra ID Connectivity for user authentication
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I would like to know if anyone has managed to connect SAP GRC 12.0 Access Control for S/4HANA to Microsoft Entra ID for user authentication for GRC guest user page. All the available documentations recommends On-premise Active directory via LDAP. However to be able to connect Entra ID makes the solution more sustainable.

I had raised a SAP Case and received a formal response that above Entra ID connectivity is possible but SAP officially yet to release help documentation for the same.

Any thoughts and guidance will help here.

Regards,

Yatin Phad

entry.title: where the smartlink in my inbox
entry.content:
Does anyone know how I can add more links to this list by clicking Material? when the documents are Purchase order

 

This document consumes the following:
CDS: C_PurOrdItemEnh:

I understand that the valuehelp of the material is the following:

Semantic object:

but I can't find how to generate that list of links and how to add one or more.

entry.title: Erro na F110 para fornecedor sem imposto
entry.content: Boa noite, estou com erro na F110. No dado mestre do fornecedor não tem imposto, e está correto pois não é para ter mesmo. Porém ao executar a F110 está ocorrendo o erro abaixo. Olhei na partida e tb não tem imposto. A proposta não teve erro. O erro acontece no pagamento. Alguém já passou por isso? Obrigada

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2024
entry.content:
Bem-vindo ao SAP Inside Track Recife 2024! Estamos entusiasmados em anunciar a nova edição deste evento incrível que reúne a comunidade SAP para compartilhar conhecimento, estreitar laços profissionais e explorar as últimas inovações no universo SAP.

 

Um Pouco da Nossa História

O SAP Inside Track teve sua primeira edição em Recife em 2016 e, desde então, tem sido um marco importante para a comunidade SAP na região. O evento faz parte de uma série global presente em muitos países, sendo organizado por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o intuito de promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e fortalecer conexões profissionais.

No Brasil, o SAP Inside Track já acumula mais de 10 anos de existência, passando por 11 cidades diferentes em 40 edições pelo país. Cidades como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Campinas, Salvador, Ribeirão Preto, Belo Horizonte, Curitiba, Fortaleza e, claro, Recife, já receberam o evento.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Recife é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Local e Data

Local: UNINASSAU, Joaquim Nabuco, Recife-PE
Data: 05 de Outubro de 2024

Inscrição

Maiores informações a seguir. Inscrevam-se nesta página para ficar por dentro das atualizações. 

Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade! Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma sinopse para: sapinsidetrack@gmail.com. O tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos, e o último dia para envio de propostas é em 05 de Agosto de 2024. Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe com a confirmação.

Agenda

A agenda completa do evento será divulgada em breve. Fique atento para mais informações!

Sobre Recife

Recife, a capital do estado de Pernambuco, é uma das cidades mais antigas do Brasil e a quarta mais desenvolvida entre os mercados emergentes do mundo. Destaca-se pelo maior parque tecnológico do Brasil, o Porto Digital, e pelo segundo maior polo médico do país. Com um aeroporto premiado e uma rica cultura gastronômica, Recife é um polo turístico e de negócios ideal para sediar eventos nacionais e internacionais, graças à sua infraestrutura de ponta e à vibrante cena cultural e tecnológica.

Organizadores e Patrocínio

O SAP Inside Track Recife é organizado por membros dedicados da Comunidade SAP, com apoio de diversos patrocinadores que tornam este evento possível. Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos que contribuem para o sucesso deste encontro.

Apoio: 

UNINASSAU

pos.uninassau.edu.br 

GGTI

www.ggti.org.br

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Recife 2024! Esperamos vê-lo lá para um dia repleto de aprendizado, networking e inovação.

entry.title: Re: Screen Exit for ES22 : Add Z*field to table Control
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for the detailed response. I tried these to some extent. As per your steps 1 & 2, HOW do i Edit the screen layout(SAPLES20H) & 200.Do you want me to copy to Z ones?

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC Spark to Intel migration on Solaris 10
entry.content:
Hi!

You are welcome!

When you logon to the system using the SAP GUI, access the menu System -> Status. There you will have a "details" button (looks like a magnifying glass") that will open the SAP version information.

Also at the System -> Status window, look for the server (or instance) name field. It will be like "hostname_SID_number". The "SID" is the System ID.

And you should be able to find SAP partners and consulting companies that could help you perform the migration.

The SAP Note 1054121 might help you find one.

Regards,

Isaías

entry.title: Re: Adding a Value Help for a Standard field in Custom Business Object
entry.content: Please remove tags other than SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility.

entry.title: Re: BTP forms service vs onPrem Adobe Forms
entry.content: Hello, The BTP Forms service is a cloud service hosted on SAP BTP. It can be used as an alternative to ADS based on SAP Netweaver system. So in terms of difference, you can save on a system landscape installation/maintenance. Ofcourse you can still use the Adobe Lifecycle Designer in conjunction with BTP Forms service as per documentation in https://help.sap.com/docs/forms-service-by-adobe/sap-forms-service-cf/using-adobe-livecycle-designer. Hope this helped. Regards, Kishan

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Public Cloud_Order confirmation form_change output is not triggering automatically

entry.title: Re: Alert notification in SAP S4/HANA public cloud and SAP BTP
entry.content: Added Manufacturing tag. Please remove other tags.

entry.title: Holiday on a non planned working day
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
How can I setup a time valuation to process hours on a public holiday that occurred on a day employees are not planned to work?
Requirement : Employees should be paid for all holidays even if they are not scheduled to work on the holiday.

Using the standard paid holiday time type group only generates hours for weeks which the employees are scheduled to work. But does not generate any hour for a non-work day holiday.

I have created a time valuation that filters the holidays, but I am not sure what the input time type group should be. I tried to use the recorded working time but it generates 0hr on non - work public holidays, since employees do not record hours on those days.


SAP Timesheet SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking HCM Time Management SAP Payroll and Time Change Management SAP Payroll and Time Change Management HCM (Human Capital Management)

entry.title: Scope Item not selectable in Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Good morning experts, please help me, in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), I do not have available to add the Scope Item 1GP, why?

I am doing the Define Scope activity.

DDA

CBC

Thank you for your attention, I await your comments.

entry.title: CSV lookup using CPI
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a CSV on an SFTP server that contains Employee's ID and their date of birth. Is it possible to do a lookup in CSV from CPI? E.g. send EmployeeID as request and get date of birth as a response.

I know I can create another iflow that reads this CSV regularly into a hashmap and do lookup there. However, I was trying to configure this ina single iflow, if possible.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: I need to convert the BUOM from KG to ML.
entry.content: Sap will not accept decimals or hundred thousand mls as it wants a comma space and will not accept the last zero ie: we purchase 380mls, PART ABC is in KG. the conversions we need is either 380000 mls to 1KG or 380mls to 0.38kg. How do I fix this without creating a new part. We have the 100 EC to 38KG conversion in the table but vendor invoices in mls

entry.title: How to activate CLT in several created projects is possible
entry.content:
Good morning, experts

Good morning, experts

Can you tell me if it is possible to activate CLT for several projects created in CBC (Central Business Configuration), or it has some restriction.

If the answer is YES, why I can not assign in the Deployment Target option the Customizing option, the button is disabled.

We are in the group of countries not localized by SAP, for this reason we must activate the CLT functionality.

We are from Ecuador

Attached images

Deployment Target

Thank you for your attention, I await your comments.

entry.content: So close to HANA SDA but extend to support many cloud platform. Am I right?😊

entry.title: Re: SSAM2310 Measurment Documents full load in each delta...
entry.content:
One of the following SAP note might help

https://me.sap.com/notes/3444431/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/3401678/E

3190709 - Memory optimization used in equipment oMDO causes measuring points to be deleted in delta sync

entry.title: Re: SAM 2310 : After attaching debugger Simulator shows Blank Screen
entry.content: Might help if you describe the debugging method. Are you using MDK debugger or Nativescript debugger? What does your launch.json look like?

entry.title: How to change destination path in BFC CONSO Development
entry.content:
After sending the objects from BFC DEV PARAM to BFC DEV CONSO, we scan the objects in CONSO. But the destination path from "scan objects" is redirected to Production BFC. Please let us know where and how to change the destination path in BFC.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change destination path in BFC CONSO Development
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Please follow these steps:

Tool - > General Options -> Transfer -> Transfer Vectors -> Network -> Configure...

Enter the new path in the Network Configuration.

 

Now your scan tasks will scan the new path.

Best Regards,

Timtouna

entry.title: Re: Manage characterstics as custom fields in Fiori apps

Rodolfo

entry.title: Create URL Attachment on Maintenance Order using BAPI ABAP
entry.content:
Hi need your help,

my requirement is to create/update maintenance order using custom program and create/update URL attachment as well, i have been searching for a while and i can't find any BAPI to help me create URL attachment at all.

Previously i tried BAPI BDS_BUSINESSDOCUMENT_CRE_O_URL  but it could only create attachment as text not URL, what i really need it URL attachment, whereby when user view list attachment they can directly click and redirect to browser to view the web page.

Please help...

entry.title: SOLMAN RFC READ ROLE
entry.content:
We have noted some discrepancies between our copy of the READ role and the SAP Standard version. I compared the release notes/log from 

I did some analysis and these are the two auth objects/values that we lose if we overwrite our role with the standard role which are undocumented.

SAP_SOLMAN_READ_702 

S_ADMI_FCD – Missing NADM

S_SDCC_ADD – Missing WRITE

 

ZS:SM:SAP_SOLMAN_READ_702

 

S_ADMI_FCD – Has NADM

S_SDCC_ADD – Has WRITE

Were these auths removed from SAP Standard READ RFC role at some point and not documented?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SOLMAN RFC READ ROLE
entry.content:
Hi customer,

Regarding your query, kindly let you know below:

As the name SAP_SOLMAN_READ_702  suggests, it is only for read purposes and should never contain write or admin. You mentioned write and admin authorizations in COPIED role. Customers can always copy roles and adjust them, but as per standard we do not ship these values for the authorization objects.

However the customer can copy the role and adjust authorizations.

Hope above help address your concern.

Sincerely,

SAP Support
